### Top 7 Vetta Sports competitors

## Compare Vetta Sports to Competitors

The National Dodgeball League is a sports league that focuses on the game of dodgeball. It organizes events, including tournaments and championships, for professional and amateur athletes. It is based in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

The Association of Pickleball Professionals is a tour sanctioned by USA Pickleball, focusing on professional, senior professional, and amateur pickleball events. It organizes a series of tournaments, including international and collegiate events, and works on initiatives for youth in pickleball. It provides rankings for players and organizes charity events. It was founded in 2019 and is based in Chicago, Illinois. [...] Scala Sports develops white-label applications for racket sports associations within the sports technology industry. Their offerings include platforms for organizing leagues, tournaments, events, and communities. They provide integrations for member databases, rating systems, court bookings, and payment processing. It was founded in 2017 and is based in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

Image 31: Rated 2 out of 5 stars

## It's really unfortunate that I need to leave
It's really unfortunate that I have to leave this company. When I first got pieces/bought older versions off of others, they were high quality and still hold up. 

Lately I've had quality issues left and right and it sucks because I haven't found an alternative to Vetta. [...] Image 1: Trustpilot reviews

results.content:
Partnership Disclosure: This blog post was not sponsored by any of the brands listed above. As always, all opinions are my own. This post may contain affiliate links, in which I earn a commission on purchases.

clothing review sustainable fashion

## 2 Replies to “Vetta Capsule Review”

1. The leatherette skirt is a great choice! Have you seen the Timeless Capsule? The Two Piece Sleeveless Dress is really speaking to me.
2. Yes! The Timeless Capsule looks lovely, especially the two piece dress like you mentioned. I ended up snagging the re-stocked Convertible Trench, which I’m excited to review soon!

Up Next:   Lady White Co vs Everlane Premium Tee [...] Though they’ve launched a few new collections this year, I appreciate that they typically only make small modifications or updates to existing capsules each time they relaunch. For example, the same blazer dress, sweater and pants were featured in last year’s Edgy Capsule too. I decided to give a few pieces from this capsule, plus their reversible midi skirt a try. While the latter is simple and can be sewed, I have no desire to fiddle with sewing silk on the bias cut :)

Now, onto the reviews:
